fund.name: HDFC Flexi Cap Fund - Growth Option - Direct Plan

allocations.rationale: This fund invests across large, mid, and small companies, giving you broad exposure to India's growth. It has delivered strong returns over 5 years with moderate volatility. At 1% yearly fee, it's fairly priced for a top-performing fund. Since you're just starting, one solid fund is enough to begin with.

fund.name: Motilal Oswal Nifty Midcap 150 Index Fund - Direct Plan
fund.fund_house: Motilal Oswal Asset Management Company Limited

allocations.rationale: Midcap stocks have higher growth potential than large companies. This index fund tracks 150 mid-sized Indian companies at a very low 0.2% yearly fee. It adds growth tilt to your portfolio while keeping costs low. It's simple and transparent — you always know what you own.

plan.setup_phase:
Step 1: Download Groww or Kuvera app and complete KYC (takes 5 minutes with PAN and bank account).
Step 2: Search for 'HDFC Flexi Cap Fund' and start a monthly SIP of ₹4,000.
Step 3: Search for 'Motilal Oswal Nifty Midcap 150 Index Fund' and start a monthly SIP of ₹1,000.
Step 4: Set up auto-debit on salary day so investing happens without you thinking about it.
review_checkpoints: After 6 months: Check if both SIPs are running smoothly.
After 1 year: Compare your funds' performance with similar funds on the app. No need to switch unless one is clearly underperforming.
Every 2 years: Rebalance if one fund grows to more than 10% above its target share.
plan.rebalancing_guidelines: Once every 2 years, check your portfolio. If the Flexi Cap fund grows to more than 90% of your total value or the Midcap Index fund goes above 30%, sell a little from the overgrown one and buy the other to bring back to 80:20. This keeps your risk balanced.
plan.rationale: At 22, you have 38 years to grow your money — that's your biggest advantage. Starting early means even small amounts can grow into large sums. You want simplicity, so we're using just two funds: one broad-market fund (80%) and one high-growth midcap fund (20%). This mix gives strong growth with manageable ups and downs. Since you're on the new tax regime, we're not using ELSS, and we're avoiding slab-taxed debt funds which give poor post-tax returns for young investors.
risks: Stock markets can drop 20-30% in a bad year — your portfolio value will fall temporarily. This is normal. Don't panic and stop investing.
Midcap funds are more volatile than large caps — they can fall harder but also rise faster.
Inflation can eat into returns if long-term gains don't beat rising prices.
Your job and income may change — keep SIPs affordable so you don't have to stop.
plan.disclaimer: For research/educational purposes only. Not certified financial advice.

sip_step_up.description: Increase your total SIP by 10% every year, starting next April. For example, ₹5,000 → ₹5,500 → ₹6,050. This matches salary hikes and builds wealth faster.

pqs.reasoning: The plan shows strong goal alignment by addressing the investor's desire for simplicity and habit-building while acknowledging the long horizon (38 years). It aligns well with the 'new_regime' tax status by avoiding 80C instruments like ELSS. It provides 'rudimentary' diversification (domestic large/mid cap) but lacks international/geographic or sector diversification, hence a high-but-not-perfect score. Risk return is appropriate for a moderate investor with a long horizon, and the internal logic (rationale matching allocations) is consistent throughout.
